text:
Once upon a moonlit night, inside a cozy little bookstore nestled at the edge of a sleepy town, a peculiar event took place. The shelf displaying fairy tales had suddenly come alive.

There stood Lady Flummox, with wings made entirely of sequins. She clapped her wings together and giggled as she danced from shelf to shelf. The tales sparkled, glowing with an inner light.

The resident librarian, Mr. Jenkins, was baffled. He'd sworn off reading fairy tales ever since that time his bedhead looked just like Sleeping Beauty. But Lady Flummox was an enchantress, bringing joy with each fluttery flight.

She picked out a tale about Cinderella who danced through enchanted forests. Suddenly, all the books started spinning around in delicate whirlwinds. Lady Flummox perched on one.

"I've got something wonderful," she said, reading aloud. "Forget about dusty old plots. How about a story where your shoes fly you wherever you want to go
